errors.lockedPeriod: This date falls within a locked period and cannot be modified.
errors.transactionDateBeforeInitialDate: Transaction date cannot be before the system initial date.
errors.initialDateConflict: The initial date cannot be set to this value because existing transactions or the locked period date conflict with it.
errors.duplicateCode: An account with this code already exists.
errors.duplicateTransactionNumber: A transaction with this number already exists for the selected type.
errors.duplicateTransactionTypeName: A transaction type with this name already exists.
errors.accountNotDeletable: This account has transactions or child accounts and cannot be deactivated.
errors.thirdPartyNotDeactivatable: This third party has active transactions and cannot be deactivated.
errors.thirdPartyRequired: A third party is required for this account.
errors.thirdPartyNotFound: The specified third party was not found.
errors.transactionTypeInUse: This transaction type is in use by existing transactions and cannot be deleted.
errors.transactionNotBalanced: Transaction items are not balanced. Debits must equal credits.
errors.nominalAccountCannotBeInactivated: This account is configured as a nominal account and cannot be deactivated. Remove it from the nominal accounts configuration first.
errors.profitLossAccountCannotBeInactivated: This account is configured as the P&L account and cannot be deactivated. Update the configuration first.
errors.closingTransactionTypeNotConfigured: No closing transaction type is configured. Please set a closing transaction type in the Setup page before executing a closing.
errors.unknownError: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

config.nominalAccountsHint: Select accounts whose balances are zeroed out on period closure
config.nominalAccountsIntroduction: Nominal accounts (revenue and expense accounts) are temporary accounts whose balances are transferred to the profit & loss account during period closure. You must select which accounts in your chart of accounts should be treated as nominal accounts, and specify a real account (asset, liability, or equity) that will receive the periodic profit or loss transfer.
config.nominalAccountsExample: Example: If your nominal accounts include Sales Revenue and Operating Expenses, their combined net balance will be transferred to a Retained Earnings or Net Income account during closure.
config.profitLossAccountExplanation: This is a real account (typically a balance sheet account) that accumulates the periodic net income or loss from the nominal accounts.
config.atLeastOneNominalAccount: At least one nominal account is required
config.closingTransactionTypeHint: Select the transaction type used when executing period closures
config.closingTransactionTypeIntroduction: When you execute a period closure, the system automatically creates closing journal entries to transfer nominal account balances to the P&L account. You must specify which transaction type should be used to classify these automatic closing transactions.
config.closingTransactionTypeExample: Example: You might create a dedicated 'Closing Entry' or 'Period Closure' transaction type to clearly identify and group all closing transactions for audit and reporting purposes.

transactionForm.initialDateNotConfiguredWarning: The system initial date must be configured in Setup before transactions can be entered.
transactionForm.dateBeforeInitialDate: Date must be on or after the system initial date ({{ date }}).

closing.closingDescription: Close out nominal accounts and post period results to the P&L account.
closing.closingProcess: This process will create a closing transaction that transfers nominal account balances to the P&L account, preparing your accounts for the next period.
closing.prerequisiteClosingTransactionType: Closing transaction type is not configured.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description:
Here's how to get your accounting system up and running:

1. Create a Tenant
Start by creating your first tenant. This isolates your accounting data. Click "Create Your First Tenant" on the main screen and fill in your organization details.

2. Set the System Initial Date
Once in your tenant, go to Setup → Accounting Configuration and set your system initial date. This marks the beginning of your accounting period.

3. Create Your Chart of Accounts
Go to Accounts and create your account structure. Start with main categories (Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Income, Expenses) and add sub-accounts as needed. Remember to use unique account codes.

4. Create Transaction Types
Go to Transaction Types and create the types of transactions your business uses (e.g., Invoice, Payment, Transfer, Journal Entry).

5. Enter Initial Balances (Optional)
If you're starting with existing account balances, go to Initial Balances and record the opening balance for each account.

6. Record Transactions
Go to Transactions and start recording your business transactions. Each transaction must balance (total debits = total credits).

7. Review Balances and Reports
Go to the Accounting page to see your account tree with current balances. Use the Reports section to view period reports, balance-at-date, and balance-by-level.

That's it! You're now ready to manage your accounting records.

tenants.title: Tenants
tenants.description: A tenant is an isolated instance of your accounting data. The system is designed to support multiple organizations or business units, each with their own separate accounts, transactions, and financial records. Each tenant operates independently with no data sharing between tenants. This ensures complete data isolation and privacy for each business unit you manage.

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.description: Accounts are the fundamental components of your chart of accounts. Each account tracks money flows in a specific area of your business. Accounts have a code (unique identifier), a name, and a debit or credit side. Accounts are organized hierarchically with parent-child relationships. You can create accounts at multiple levels to represent different areas of your business—for example, Assets with sub-accounts for Cash, Receivables, and Equipment.

accountChart.title: Account Chart
accountChart.description:
The Account Chart is the hierarchical structure of all your accounts. It organizes accounts into groups and sub-groups, making it easy to navigate and understand your financial structure. Common account hierarchies include:

• Assets (Current Assets, Fixed Assets)
• Liabilities (Current Liabilities, Long-term Liabilities)
• Equity
• Income/Revenue
• Expenses

The chart can be customized to match your business needs. Nominal accounts (income and expense accounts) are used for period reporting and can be included in period closure simulations.

initialBalances.title: Initial Balances
initialBalances.description: Initial balances are the opening balances for your accounts at a given point in time. Before recording regular transactions, you establish the starting balance for each account. This is typically done when setting up the system or when beginning a new accounting period. You specify a system initial date, and all initial balance transactions must be dated on or after this date. Initial balances ensure that your account balances reflect the state of your accounts at the beginning of your chosen period.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.description: A transaction is a business event that affects one or more accounts. Every transaction follows the principle of double-entry bookkeeping: money flows from one account (debit) to another account (credit). Each transaction must balance—total debits must equal total credits. Transactions are recorded with a date, type, description, and a list of line items specifying accounts and amounts. You can also optionally assign a third party to a transaction to track interactions with external organizations.

prefilledCharts.title: Pre-filled Charts
prefilledCharts.description: Select a pre-filled chart of accounts template and merge it into your tenant's chart. Existing accounts are skipped automatically.
prefilledCharts.name: Name
prefilledCharts.mergeConfirm: This will create all accounts from the selected template that don't already exist in your chart. Existing accounts will be skipped. This action is safe to run multiple times.
